preamble: Fortition of the velar fricative [ɣ] to the voiced velar plosive [g] is a phonological process where a continuant strengthens to an obstruent. This process occurs both diachronically in language change and synchronically in certain phonological environments, representing a reversal of the typical lenition pathway.

examples.note: Fortition of intervocalic fricatives in word-initial and post-consonantal positions during early Germanic evolution.
Strengthening of velar fricatives in certain dialects and in borrowing contexts, particularly before stressed vowels.
Historical and dialectal fortition in word-initial position and post-consonantally in some Dutch varieties.
